topic: barrel size and wine-to-oak surface ratio
trigger_keywords: mayor superficie de contacto y aporte de roble

steps.label: cause
steps.text: The same volume of wine is divided among smaller rather than larger oak vessels.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: A smaller barrel provides more wood surface area relative to the volume of wine, increasing contact with oak and oxygen transfer per litre.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Oak-derived flavour and structural influence are generally more pronounced and develop more quickly than in a larger vessel.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Oak-derived flavour and structural influence are generally more pronounced and develop more quickly than in a larger vessel.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
